title: Pilosella norrliniiformis
taxonomy: Pilosella norrliniiformis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe. It is classified within the family Asteraceae. The species is further divided into the genus Pilosella, which belongs to the tribe Cichorieae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships. Pilosella norrliniiformis is closely related to other Pilosella species, sharing similarities in their leaf and flower structures.
uses: Pilosella norrliniiformis is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Scandinavia. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal benefits and potential side effects. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.

intro: Pilosella norrliniiformis, also known as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy family, is a type of plant commonly found in the mountainous regions of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella norrliniiformis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Pilosella norrliniiformis is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or salve, and is used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
